llm_result.summary: The case reflects recurrent brief panic spikes with chest tightness, fear of fainting in public, and social-evaluative worry, maintained by safety behaviors (pulse checks, escape planning) and heightened attention to internal sensations. A modifiable set of BIO/PSYCHO/SOCIAL levers targeting interoceptive arousal, cognitive appraisals, safety-behavior reduction, social exposure, and lifestyle factors is proposed to reduce episode frequency/severity, improve perceived control, and enhance public functioning through graded exposure, cognitive techniques, and stability-building behaviors.

solutions.solution_candidate: A structured 8–12 week program combining interoceptive exposure to panic-like sensations (e.g., brief dizziness, rapid breathing, lightheadedness) with cognitive restructuring to challenge catastrophic beliefs (eg, imminent fainting) and prevention of safety behaviors (pulse checking, escape planning) in real-world settings.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reduces vulnerability to abrupt arousal by re-sensitizing the person to bodily sensations, reframing perceived threat, and decreasing reliance on safety behaviors that sustain anxiety.
solutions.measurement: Episodes_per_week; Peak_anxiety_onset_0-10; Pulse_check_frequency_per_episode; Escape_planning_frequency_per_episode; Belief_fainting_rating_0-10; Social_evaluation_tension_0-10
solutions.safety_notes: No safety concerns; implement with clinician guidance; monitor for initial distress during exposures.

solutions.solution_candidate: An 8-week mindfulness-based program emphasizing breath-focused practice, body scan, nonjudgmental awareness, and decentering from bodily sensations to reduce reactivity to interoceptive cues and distress tolerance. Integrate brief daily practice and in-session formal exercises.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Nonjudgmental awareness reduces catastrophic interpretation and emotional reactivity to internal cues, thereby lowering the intensity and perceived threat of episodes and social worry.

solutions.solution_candidate: A structured plan to identify safety behaviors (pulse checking, exit planning) and implement exposure with response prevention, including urge-surfing for arousal and progressive withdrawal of safety supports.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Diminishing safety behaviors reduces negative reinforcement of anxiety and minimizes attention to internal cues, supporting tolerance and control.
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor distress during exposure; adjust pace as needed

solutions.solution_candidate: Urge-awareness training with alternative coping strategies (paced breathing, grounding, or outward cues) to reduce immediate pulse-checking during episodes.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Replacing automatic monitoring with adaptive coping reduces salience of bodily signals and slows the escalation of panic.
solutions.safety_notes: Ensure coping strategies are safe and accessible in public settings

solutions.solution_candidate: A graded public-exposure plan with in-session coaching, starting in low-stakes environments and progressing to more crowded settings, paired with social skills practice and breathing/self-talk strategies.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Repeated social exposure reduces fear of evaluation, increases perceived control, and lowers unpredictability by creating predictable experiences.
solutions.safety_notes: Clinical supervision recommended; tailor pace to comfort levels

solutions.solution_candidate: Cognitive restructuring focused on catastrophic beliefs (eg, 'I will faint in public') with behavioral experiments to test predictability and control, using Socratic questioning and evidence gathering.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Directly targets exaggerated threat appraisals and perceived lack of control, reducing avoidance and future symptom escalation.
solutions.safety_notes: May provoke distress; monitor and adjust.

solutions.solution_candidate: Interventions to reduce embarrassment concerns through self-compassion training, group normalization of symptoms, and social coaching to improve belonging and reduce self-criticism.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reducing shame and increasing self-acceptance lowers social avoidance and fear of judgment, improving social engagement.
solutions.safety_notes: Group-format safety considerations; ensure supportive environment

solutions.solution_candidate: Attention-training to anchor gaze outward during onset (environmental scanning tasks, 3-object grounding) to decrease rapid internal monitoring.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Shifting attention away from internal sensations reduces symptom salience and arousal amplification.

solutions.solution_candidate: Sleep hygiene program with fixed bed/wake times, caffeine reduction, and a simple wind-down routine; track with a sleep diary to improve baseline arousal and autonomic regulation.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Better sleep reduces baseline arousal and improves autonomic stability, lowering panic susceptibility and daytime dysregulation.
solutions.safety_notes: If sleep complaints persist, consider medical evaluation

solutions.solution_candidate: Moderate-intensity aerobic program (3–4 days/week, 20–40 minutes) with progression and breathing control, aimed at reducing baseline autonomic arousal and improving resilience.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Regular aerobic activity modulates autonomic tone and reduces panic reactivity; improves energy and coping.
solutions.safety_notes: Medical clearance if risk factors; monitor signals during progression

solutions.solution_candidate: Use a wearable to monitor HRV and skin conductance with real-time feedback; train in paced breathing and autonomic regulation during episodes and daily life.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Biofeedback enhances conscious control of arousal and helps decouple arousal from catastrophic interpretations.
solutions.safety_notes: Data privacy considerations; not a substitute for clinical care

solutions.solution_candidate: Structured social support: reconnect with trusted friends/family, engage in community social groups (social prescribing), and implement a 4–8 week check-in plan for ongoing companionship and accountability.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Enhanced belonging and practical support buffer against social embarrassment and unpredictability, reducing avoidance and episode frequency.
solutions.safety_notes: Respect boundaries; ensure inclusive and supportive groups
